reviews.text:
Half-a-decade ago, I urgently needed an attorney during a very challenging life event that I wouldn't wish on anyone. First time ever, in a difficult unexpected situation of such nature. I had blood-related family on my side and I needed legal representation. From a very short list of attorneys, I chose Curtis D Colaw.

After all these years, I am still very grateful that I had Curtis Colaw as my attorney. I have zero regrets! That matters significantly and it is because of how Curtis handled affairs as well as the guidance he gave me on choices, options and decisions we made. I know my mental and emotional state at the time, and it is very clear to me that I would have regrets today if Curtis did not have my best interest at heart.
Thank you very much Curtis!
You have an amazing staff as well.

And yes, I'll call Curtis D. Colaw again if I ever need legal representation.
Garbage attorney He did not make it to trial and Case was dismissed. Highly do not recommend, I paid over $1200 for nothing and Cutis is refusing a refund. Fraud and shady business. Go elsewhere!!

reviews.text:
My mom paid him to have my grandma's property transferred to her trust after my grandma died. We just found out 8 years later he didn't put the deed in her trust. And guess what... we've been trying to get a hold of him for a week and he won't return a call. I guess the whole office operates the same way. After talking to the county clerks office, they confirmed he is not held in very high regard in the area. It makes me sick taking advantage of people who have already had a loss. Check his Yelp reviews!

Update: Turns out just this morning he emailed my mom, even though she had called multiple times asking for a call back. He told her he "filed the deed this morning and closed her case"! Without any correspondence! Unfortunately since he couldn't be bothered to return her call to see what she was trying to tell him, he didn't file it correctly! What a joke.

reviews.text: Attorney Jordan is a very professional person who helped me in a case where he reduced a serious charge against me, then had it dismissed. 100% recommended.

I found Jared Jordan through my own research, and after reading his glowing reviews, I reached out in hope—and I’m so glad I did. Everything written about him is absolutely accurate: Jared is an attorney of the highest caliber.

From the first call, he brought something rare to the table: a combination of calm compassion, razor-sharp strategy, and unwavering strength. I was facing an incredibly painful, high-stakes legal situation—one that could have spiraled into lasting damage for me and my family. Jared not only believed in me, but made me feel protected. He never made me feel dramatic or dismissed. Instead, he treated me with respect, empathy, and clarity.

What impressed me most was how strategic and transparent he was. He walked me through multiple possible outcomes and laid out a plan for each one, so I always knew where I stood. That clarity was grounding. That care was priceless. Thanks to Jared’s guidance, the charges were dropped, and I was exonerated. But what will stay with me even longer is how empowered and heard I felt throughout the process.

A special note of thanks to Jenna in his office, who was an absolute joy to work with. She was professional, kind, and incredibly quick to respond—every email, every document, every question was handled with grace and precision. When you’re going through something traumatic, that kind of support makes a huge difference. Jenna’s warmth and efficiency perfectly reflected the integrity and heart this firm brings to its clients.

If you’re facing a legal crisis and want someone who will fight smart, lead with heart, and make you feel like you matter, I cannot recommend Jared Jordan enough. He is everything you could want in an attorney when you’re facing seemingly insurmountable challenges.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Thank you so much for taking the time to share such a powerful and heartfelt review. Your words truly mean a great deal to both me and Jenna. It's deeply rewarding to know that our support made a difference for you during what was clearly a very difficult time.

From the start, our goal was to ensure you felt heard, protected, and empowered—and I’m honored to know that came through. Your strength and trust throughout the process played a vital role in the outcome, and I’m incredibly grateful to have been part of your journey toward justice and peace of mind.

reviews.text: At 52 years old & 25 years married- I had the unfortunate experience of finding my husband a sheriff deputy, having an affair with a judicial commissioner who he was the bailiff for.  This led me to Ms. Jensen's office to retain her for a divorce cycle.  At the start, I was a blubbering, terrified mess.  Ms. Jensen was confident and firm and several times- put me in check.  My case took 2 1/2 years and the timeline was not due to anything Ms. Jensen did or did not do.  My ex husband threw a wild card at every turn from not showing up to court several times & firing an attorney.  Ms. Jensen stayed the course.  She is honest and transparent.  She stays true to her own personal integrity and that keeps the cycle "clean" and it kept me sane and and realistic about expectations.  When you meet her at the courthouse, you can expect a little delay because the moment she steps out of the elevator she is like a beam of light that shoots down the hallway and everyone stops to talk to her & she stops for everyone & gives them a few minutes of her busy day.  She radiates down what I call the "Hallway of Hate"  AKA as Dept. 25 & makes things feel brighter and easier.  She is solution oriented and advocates for you in ways that are beyond the courtroom that you may not realize until you are at the end of the process.  She advocates for what is right and what is fair.  Her office staff- Jennifer, David & Anjni...they are a little village of patient, kind and nurturing people who do a lot behind the scene to keep things moving.  They ALWAYS keep you in the loop on everything & they are ALWAYS available for questions.  In the end, Ms. Jensen and her staff served me well.  I am VERY, VERY happy with my outcome- both financially & emotionally & that I owe to Ms. Jensen and her staff.  I'm not an advocate of divorce BUT if you find yourself, like I did- in a situation that it is your next step- then retain Ms. Jensen!  She's undoubtedly the best, the absolute best!

reviews.ownerResponse: If this is the case I think it is, then the defendant was prosecuted under the career criminal statute (Penal Code section 999e) which prohibits plea bargaining. A loss at trial (even a partial loss) would certainly have resulted in substantially more custody time than the judge imposed when this defendant pled guilty as charged to avoid trial. A less experienced attorney might have yielded to the temptation to go to trial.

reviews.text: I put my son's life in his hands I trusted my son's life with him and he failed us he failed us so bad he sent my son up for failure my son had never been in trouble in his life never ever Mr Baker said it was going to be a simple theft charge in it turned into two strikes and seven years after he went to lunch with the prosecutor he's changed on me he wouldn't answer my calls anymore he wouldn't tell me anything and he made my son he told my son that my son had to take that deal which he had never gotten offered not one deal in 9 months of being there with a 400,000 bail the whole time Martin Baker is horrible horrible person he is no good do not trust him after he went to lunch with the prosecutor he changed on me I don't even know how he sleeps at night they knew that my son was going to be desperate to take any deal because they went 9 months without giving him a deal a scared little boy not knowing anything about the law and Baker told him he had to take that deal to plead guilty to take two strikes and an open plea not even knowing what the sentence was going to be and he ends up getting 7 years and two strikes all because I trusted Martin Baker
reviews.ownerResponse: This client (the reviewer's son) voluntarily pled no contest to two robberies and three felony thefts after being made fully aware of the state of the evidence and the potential consequences of the plea. As I make clear on my website, the only promise I will ever make to a potential or existing client is that I will do everything in my power to ensure that they are treated fairly and the best possible outcome is attained in their case. This case was prosecuted as a "career criminal" case under Penal Code section 999e, which legally prevented the DA from entering into a plea bargain, leaving the defendant with the choice of "pleading open" or going to trial. In many cases, including this one, going to trial can present a risk of a much harsher sentence, even if one or more charges are ultimately not proven to a jury.  (By the way, I never went to lunch with anyone involved in the prosecution of this case.)

reviews.text: I supposedly made an appointment with Ana Evans and the day of my consultation they told me Roksana S. Bidgoli was the lawyer doing the consultation. I gave them my case number when I made the appointment and I guess she review all the information as soon as I spoke to her the questions she asked me didn’t make any sense like where did I get the money to pay the other lawyer, that she didn’t have a magic wand and when I asked her what was I entitled too as a marriage of 30 years she said that if I was speaking out of emotions that was not going to help me, which I didn’t understand what she meant but she treated me like if she was a Psychologist and not a lawyer. Have corporate legal plan, which sent me to this law firm with instructions to call them and set up an appointment. Did that.
When I called at the appointment time, they started to ask about my coverage, what provider it was, etc. They had not received the referral information (and it seems legalese that handles these dropped the ball in sending out the case information).
But rather than ask about this during the appointment set up time, or any time in between (there were 2 business days between the set up time and the actual appointment), they did this during the "lawyer" time. And rather than let us talk to a lawyer and have the office sort it out with the legal plan, they just kept going round and round. We spent 25 minutes forwarding our email to their office, and having them search different ways for the referral to no avail. Ended up wasting our time, the office receptionist time, the lawyer sitting idle (or having lunch?), and I'm out of legal help for a few extra days till I can set up a new appointment (which will NOT be with this firm).
They care more about money than about helping a client. I understand they need to get paid, but they should have alerted us ahead of time to be able to sort this out.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you for your input.  It is necessary to have all of the legal insurance information before the consult moves forward for many reasons. I normally don’t write reviews, but I feel it’s important to share my experience. I came to this firm hoping for guidance and genuine help with my custody case, but I walked away feeling more like a number than a client. There were key things that could’ve been brought up in court — things both the Minor’s Counsel and Co-Parenting Therapy mentioned — that were never discussed or presented by my attorney even after reading the papers and the reason why we went back to court.

Throughout my case, communication was poor. Several of my emails went unanswered, and when I asked about it, I was told it was to “save me money and to trust them.” Yet, I was still billed large amounts for things that felt unnecessary — including simple custody orders that were basically a copy and paste. At one point, I was even handed another client’s paperwork on my court day, which made me feel uncomfortable and uneasy.

To make things worse, the judge ordered certain forms to be completed back in October, and I later found out from Minor’s Counsel that those forms were never filled out by my attorney. After spending close to $20,000 (and I’m still paying it off), I honestly feel like the system — and especially this firm — cares more about billing hours than truly helping families.

For a father doing everything possible to be there for his kids, this was an incredibly disappointing experience. I wish my money and effort could’ve gone toward my children, not wasted on legal work that didn’t move my case forward. I could’ve saved an extra 5000 and just requested Minors Council instead of hiring an attorney. After three years of fighting for my daughter, I finally just gave up on attorneys and I am now trying to trust and hope that things work out.

reviews.ownerResponse: At Borton Petrini we value all clients and strive to provide personalized legal services intended to meet the goals of our clients.  Unfortunately, we cannot predict the actions or legal stances that an opposing party will take such as competing motions.  Initial retainers are based on the experience of an attorney analyzing the facts presented during the initial consultation.  Should an opposing party choose to take action to the detriment of our client, our attorneys will strenuously advocate for our client and will seek what is in the best interest for them.  The cost of legal representation can be expensive; however, we strive to keep costs down and always provide clients with options suited for their financial position.

reviews.text: To say that this attorney is amazing is an understatement. Jessica Dorn from Borton Petrini LLP has been through some pretty tough situations with our family.  Jessica took the time to really get to know our family and guide us to do what was best for our granddaughter.  We have dealt with some attorney's and always felt like they were overcharging us or not invested in us. We never felt this way with Jessica and her law firm, from the front desk to Jessica. They were always detailed and fair.  Jessica even took a Saturday out of her busy family schedule to assist us in a tough situation.  I could always count on her either answering my calls, emails or texts.  It was nice to know she had our back always and felt like a friend when dealing with her.  She cares for her job and it shows in hard work and dedication to her clients.  We couldn't have done with out her and we thank God for her every day. reviews.text: I paid a retainer and was told if my case did not go to court( which it did not) that I would more than likely get some of my money back. Before my case even went to the mediator I was charged enough to pretty much max out my retainer. Jessica overcharged me for our first meeting, that she was 20-25 minutes late for because of another meeting. Than during my time talking to her she stated theirs no magic number in which a child can live with the other parent, so I go online and lo and behold California law states that when a child is 14, the child may go in front of court to state their case on why they would like to move. I felt she was biased towards moms and I was left out of the loop while she tried talking to my exes attorney. Even though I was paying her to request what I wanted, she negated that and did her own thing. When I called her out on it she became defensive and rude. Money definitely not spent well. I wish I had went with a different attorney.

I was fortunate enough to have been represented by Jessica Dorn at Borton Petrini. I truly can't say enough good things about her and her ability as a family law attorney! Jessica has been a blessing during a difficult divorce and custody process as well as a guardianship case. She was highly recommended to me for good reason, and I would glady refer any of my friends/family to her, to assist them in such an emotional and stressful process. Not only is she knowledgable, tenacious and professional when dealing with these types of situations, but she is also kind, compassionate, understanding, empathetic, and its obvious that she truly cares about her clients and their needs. She never made me feel rushed, unheard or judged throughout this process and she has a way of taking an excessive amount of information, jumbled with opinions and emotion, and making it into a concise statement that gets the point across. reviews.ownerResponse:
"Kelly Law" - aka Kelly Widdowson.
Ms. Widdowson, you have chosen not to use your real name when making this post. It is quite revealing that you, as an opposing party in a legal matter, feel compelled to leave a negative review about our firm. The fact that you are unhappy about the outcome of the case speaks volumes and serves to strengthen that our legal arguments and application of the law resulted in a favorable finding for my client.

To address your claims:

Spelling Mistakes and Grammatical Errors: Any minor errors do not detract from the substance and effectiveness of our filings. The outcome of your case was determined by the facts and legal arguments, not typographical details.

Unprofessional Conduct and Lies: These baseless accusations are a transparent attempt to undermine our reputation. Our firm adheres to the highest ethical standards, as evidenced by our long-standing history of success and professionalism at both state and federal levels.

Promoting Other Attorneys: Ironically, the attorneys you suggest either do not practice in the area of family law and/or were the attorneys on the other side of this case or were attorneys you expressed dissatisfaction with and made disparaging comments about during the proceeding. These attorneys are my colleagues with stellar reputations.

I understand your frustration regarding the outcome of your case, and I do hope that you find peace and can heal. However, to post a review under the pretext of a false name "Kelly Law" and pretend to be someone who hired our firm out of spite is unfortunate.
The Law Office of Tracy Y. Toledo has a well-established record of effectively representing our clients and achieving favorable results. It is unfortunate that your dissatisfaction with the court’s decision has led you to post this unwarranted and unethical review.

We stand by our work and our commitment to OUR clients. You are NOT nor were you ever my client. You were an opposing party.

Nevertheless, I do wish you the best of luck in the future. And, we hold no animosity toward you for this attempt to tarnish our representation through this false post. We understand you have made this post out of anger and we do understand that family law can be very emotional. However, your post was clearly false and misleading.

Sincerely,
Law Office of Tracy Y. Toledo

reviews.text:
I found myself needing to redo a bad custody agreement/order for myself and my three year old son. Tracy Y. Toledo came highly recommended to me both by friends and her online presence. Over the course of a year, Tracy Y. Toledo, showed my family understanding, kindness, and compassion. She has a detailed expertise in matters of child custody law, and had the tact to tell me hard truths in a compassionate way, so that I would understand them.

Tracy and her Paralegal Alicia worked tirelessly for the benefit of my family, always putting the needs and welfare of my son above anything else. Unlike other law firms, Tracy is not in the business of law to make money; she does it to help struggling families. Her billing practices are very fair, and she never overbills. She gave detailed instructions on what I needed to do to gather and present all my evidence in a clear and concise way, so the court could see the real picture on what was going on, and she fiercely fought for my family in every mediation meeting we had.

Trusting Tracy with my family’s wellbeing has been one of the best decisions I have made, and I will continue to trust her with what is most important to me. I highly recommend anyone who needs child custody lawyer to contact her.

Tracy and Alicia are amazing at what they do. When in a difficult situation in life Tracy and her team made me feel at ease. They always take care of everything that comes up. With Tracy I have one less thing to worry about. Tracy actually cares about her clients. I plan on keeping Tracy as my attorney as long as she is practicing law. She is the best attorney I have ever encountered. If you need an excellent attorney I highly recommend Tracy and her entire team.

My honest opinion is that when I called to set up the appointment I was told that my child meets all criteria and that an attorney will be contacting me within a few days. Yesterday evening I received a call from a woman who introduced herself as an attorney and I do not know how true that is given the misinformation that was said while speaking. I have been in the process of getting my child’s social security started and just recently my child has been diagnosed by a psychiatrist including the documentation from the school. The woman speaking asked me a few questions pertaining to my income and asked if I received government assistance to which I answered yes. Then she proceeded to ask me if I work and I also stated yes. She then asked if I was single to which I applied yes. This lovely human being then proceeded to state that Social security will not qualify my child because of my income and that if they do approve my child that the amount would be set to $0. She grouped everyone’s cases to together to prove her point by stating her and her husband have been doing this for years and multiple people wasted their time. She also stated to wait and reapply when my child is over the age of 18 because social security will not pay because I have a income. I just wanted to state how much trash spewed from this woman’s mouth and it is disgusting to project misinformation on someone who is seeking assistance. I’ve talked to countless parents who work 2 jobs and their child still qualifies and receives social security benefits. This woman sounds wacked out of her mind to think a determined parent would even consider what she has to say as being truthful. Sounds biased and untrained to me and very unprofessional to group every case they’ve had into one category because every case is not the same whether it’s for social security or not everyone has their different reasons on being approved or denied and it’s definitely not based on income. This Wacked out attorney made it seem like I was sitting on thousands of dollars a month yes I work more than one job and I’m in school but Im also a single mother of 3 children and have a household of bills. I would hope I have a income Wacked out lady. Save yourself some time and speak with a reputable and knowledgeable attorney these people have no clue what’s going on or what they’re talking about.
Very happy with all there help  made it so easy for me

Yo no les recomiendo a este abogado. El señor es un charlatán que te dice cosas que se oyen bien pero al ultimo no hace lo que dice. Con cuidado porque pueden perder sus derechos o libertad con este tipo de actitud. Yo perdí mis derechos y los policías siguen viniendo a mi casa por poner música Mexicana cuando limpio la yarda.

In my immigration case, my first attempt without a lawyer I was denied because I did not submit certified court documents. Patrick also did not submit all the right documentation.

The civil case he never should have accepted because he is not a civil litigation lawyer. I trusted him to take on the case after having a consultation with him. He said "I know Judge John Mayne in civil court, we go out to eat." "He's fair and looks at all sides" which was not the case. Judge Mayne is prejudiced and does not hide it.

We have a hostile neighbor couple who calls the cops on us whenever we play Mexican music. In the last 2 years they have filed multiple restraining orders on me. That's why I decided to hire a lawyer to help. After accepting payment he didn't even bother to read the statements. He said "I only skimmed through them" when I asked him if he read them.

One of the hostile neighbors is a retired sheriff, who I witnessed choke someone out in the alley. I gave Patrick the police report number and he never submitted a request. This same person was arrested for assaulting another neighbor, which is why my family has been concerned by his behavior.

I would not trust Patrick on a serious case where you could  get deported or a case that involves losing your rights. I would not have hired him for my immigration case because if it would have gone to trial there was no way he was going to win.

edit: *update Jan 8, 2025*

Cuidado con este señor no es un hombre de palabra. No protegió a mi family ni mis derechos. Es amigo del juez, y le tuvo miedo a mis vecinos porque es un sherif retirado. Lleven testigos cuando hablen con el porque les cambia la historia. Tomen notas. No lo recomiendo. Hay mejores abogados que si son honestos.

My review is to inform people who might consider hiring you. I’m not looking for your response to clarify things and I wont play your lawyer games. You clearly spent more time on your response than you did defending my family.

After court, you told my wife and I how Judge Mayne was unfair and how he would have ruled the case different if the music I played was in English.

Patrick, the judge himself disclosed that you guys are friends and go out to eat. It was the first thing he said in court. Judge John R Mayne said “Mr Kolasisnski and I go out to eat...are the Parkers ok with that?” They responded “yes.” That was said in court with everyone there. You said it to me in your office during the consultation and he repeated it in court. Court is open to the public I wasn’t the only one who heard the judge say that.

Your advice was for me to wear a headset when I do yard work.

I consulted with a local civil attorney and discussed all the details in the case. They couldn’t understand why you handled the case the way you did. There are also more personal details about you that I can’t share for legal reasons.

Im not afraid to speak up on your antics, or my neighbors hostile attitude toward my wife and kids, and the corrupt court. We all know how they side with their sheriffs. They act like can do no wrong even though the country has witnessed their behavior. The RO was to protect my wife and kids, you did not do what you said you would. Even now in your response you did not mention what you did do to defend my family or my rights, because you didn’t do anything.

The images I uploaded are all information that was submitted to the court. It is all public record.

Moving forward, out of all of the lawyers I have hired in my life you are by far the worst.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Andres, I'm sorry your case didn't work out as expected, and I won't get into the details of why out of respect for your privacy, but this review is more than a little inaccurate.

I have never, and would never, suggest that I have a special connection with any Judge. I did say that I know Judge Mayne to be fair, which he is, and frankly, was in your case.

We do the best we can with the facts we have. Facts came out during your hearing that you hadn't shared before, and it went poorly. I'm sorry we couldn't work a miracle, but there's only so much we can do in the situation you were in, especially when you don't listen to the advice your lawyer gives you.

We did manage to successfully resolve your immigration case, which is why you hired me for the restraining order. I'm sorry that didn't work out, but you also have never expressed the sentiments here to me. When we walked out of court, you thanked me for my work and accepted why the case went the way it did. If you would like to meet and discuss the case, I'm always here.

I recently had the opportunity to witness Attorney Eugene Kilbride in the courtroom, and even though it was the first time I saw him in action, the experience left an indelible mark on me. Kilbride wasn't even representing me, yet his advocacy made me feel a profound sense of safety and assurance. His presence and command over the courtroom were extraordinary, showcasing a blend of powerful eloquence and unwavering dedication that inspired not only me but undoubtedly others present.

Kilbride’s arguments before the judge were nothing short of impressive. His ability to articulate complex legal points with clarity and precision left me in awe. It's rare to see someone so in control, seemingly anticipating every angle and presenting them with such clarity that even complicated matters seemed straightforward. Observing him, I felt a sense of comfort knowing there are attorneys out there capable of such dedication and formidability in defense of their clients.

I quickly asked Mr Kilbride for a business card and with a big smile, he provided me with one.

Intrigued by what I witnessed, I decided to delve deeper into Kilbride’s professional background. A quick online search confirmed my initial impression—his reputation is sterling. Not only have numerous clients praised his professional expertise, but his firm is also consistently recommended for its exceptional service. Testimonials from past clients speak volumes about his thorough approach and empathetic understanding, qualities that set him apart as a leader in his field.

While I haven't had the pleasure of working with him yet, the prospect of potentially having Mr. Kilbride on my side is reassuring. His courtroom prowess, coupled with an apparent dedication to his clients, makes me hopeful that he can bring favorable outcomes in challenging situations.

In conclusion, observing Attorney Eugene Kilbride in the courtroom was more than just witnessing legal proceedings; it was an inspirational experience. His commendable approach, both in and out of the courtroom, exemplifies what it means to be an advocate. I am optimistic about the possibility of collaborating with him on my legal matters, confident that his skill and dedication will prove invaluable. For anyone in need of a committed and powerful attorney, Eugene Kilbride certainly stands out as a top choice.

May God bless his work and continue to guide his efforts in supporting and defending his clients with such vigor and integrity

-Eduardo Avila

reviews.text:
I have been very impressed with Seth Hanson Law.  Bankruptcy is stressful and my case was somewhat more challenging because I have been based overseas for some time.  SHL was my third or fourth try at finding a firm that knew how to handle my circumstances.

From the get go in my dealings with Spencer and Crystal to them setting me up with a call with Mr. Hanson and then to my more in-depth dealings with my case manager Chris; the whole process was handled professionally, efficiently and with the utmost care.

I was very reassured that Mr. Hanson had experience with international clients in CA bankruptcy cases.  He showed a command of my particular situation that I couldn't even get through the front door with the other firms. I had tried doing research on my own as well and as a layman, found it very complex and confusing.  Frankly, it didn’t seem feasible until I engaged with SHL.

I spent the most time with Chris going through all the steps and information required.  He was very detail oriented and methodical.  He too had experience with clients in bankruptcy who are based overseas. Things like exchange rates and different tax regulations can make the process a little more tricky.  But Chris worked alongside me and always came back to me promptly when and where he needed more information.  He is an excellent communicator and stayed on top of things in a timely manner.

I feel that my case is in the best possible hands with Seth Hanson Law.  Thank you very much.

Life-Changing Experience – Highly Recommend Tom Hogan & His Team

We came to Tom Hogan’s office during one of the most difficult and stressful times in our lives. After being let down over and over again by a previous attorney who failed to represent me properly and left my case in a mess, we were incredibly blessed to come across Tom and his team — truly by the grace of God.

From the very first consultation, which lasted over 3.5 hours, Tom and his staff were engaged, thorough, and fully committed to helping me. They immediately got to work, reviewing everything, untangling the complications of my case, and preparing the right motions to fix the damage that had been done before. Since then, Tom and his amazing team of paralegals have consistently communicated with me, kept me in the loop, and treated my case with genuine care and urgency. I can say with full confidence that they are invested in fighting for what’s right — not just going through the motions. They want to help me move forward with my life, and that means everything.

If you’re going through a tough divorce or you feel unheard, lost, or unsupported by your current attorney — I strongly encourage you to reach out to Tom Hogan. You will be heard. You will be supported. And you can trust them to fight for you. I am beyond grateful for this team. Tom is, without a doubt, the best divorce attorney in the area. I would recommend him 1000 times over.

reviews.text: Just be careful I payed full amount for my case he wanted me to settle off court which I politely didn’t not agree I want to go in front of a judge in which he drop me a day before my court day now all of a sudden his office is emailing me saying I owe money  just be careful do your research with him
reviews.ownerResponse:
We take all feedback seriously and are committed to upholding the highest standards of professionalism and client service.

To provide clarity regarding your matter, we would like to respectfully address your statements. On April 21, 2025, we personally communicated with you regarding your retainer. In an effort to accommodate your financial situation, we voluntarily reduced your retainer requirement from $2,500 to $1,500. During this conversation, you stated, “Thank you, Tom, for everything you have done, but unfortunately, I am unable to replenish.” We had also come to an agreement for a lesser monthly payment; however, you ultimately chose not to replenish your retainer and elected to represent yourself in court. This decision, in turn, resulted in a higher monthly payment moving forward. At your request, we proceeded to file a Substitution of Attorney.

Our office made several good faith efforts to contact you regarding outstanding issues and your account balance. When we were finally able to reach you on June 16, 2025, you advised that you would not be paying the remaining balance and directed us to pursue the matter in small claims court and hung up.

We wish to clarify that the final invoice reflects services that were rendered and that you were made aware in advance of the possibility of an outstanding balance. The assertion that charges were unexpected or issued “all of a sudden” is not accurate.

Our firm is committed to exceeding client expectations. In your case, we extended a gesture of goodwill by gifting you a signed baseball lithograph from a renowned artist.

We remain dedicated to transparency, fairness, and the ethical practice of law. Should you wish to discuss your concerns in further detail, we invite you to contact our office directly.

reviews.text: Everyone here at the Law office of Thomas Hogan are amazing! Truly compassionate and understanding. I find Mr. Hogan has great compassion for our community. He help our family by steering us in the right direction. We truly have so much gratitude.

This man is incredibly unprofessional. He didn’t give me a chance to tell him what was happening in my case. He continuously cut me off, jumped to conclusions, told me I didn’t have things that I had brought, etc. I previously had a consultation with another attorney that said my case was serious, that I needed to get sole custody, that a judge would agree and what evidence to gather. I thought his CPA background would be helpful so I wanted to have a second consultation before retaining someone. It ended with him yelling at me, throwing papers and storming out of the consultation I had just paid $300 for. His paralegal sat with us and was helpful and his receptionist was very apologetic. The whole this was absolutely wild.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Thank you for sharing your feedback about your consultation experience with our office. We apologize that our meeting did not meet your expectations. However, we would like to clarify that our attorney and team made every effort to assist you during your consultation. ​He actually gave you a free consultation. Our attorney reviewed documents unrelated to the consultation, demonstrating our commitment to understanding your case. ​You would not listen to the attorney's advice. The attorney even offered to specially appear ​at your upcoming hearing​ to which you said no to. Both you and your mother were extremely rude so our office staff stepped in to assist in the best way possible.

We understand that the consultation may not have ended as either of us had hoped. We appreciate the kind words about our paralegal and receptionist, who were helpful and apologetic.

We wish you the best in finding the right representation for your needs.

Thomas P Hogan

I couldn't be more pleased with the exceptional service I received from Tom Hogan and his team. From the moment I reached out, they were responsive, professional, and genuinely cared about my case. Tom provided some insight to us that was valuable today.  Advice rather and additional information.  I thought I'd share this experience!  I am sure in the future our son can reach out for advice if needed and Tom and team would be helpful and our son can the work, but pay Tom for advice because he was helpful today and took his time to sit with us where we ran into one another.

I consulted with three different attorneys but lacked confidence in their responses. After conducting further research, I discovered the law office of Thomas P. Hogan. From the moment I began speaking with Mr. Hogan and his paralegal, I realized he was the ideal attorney for my complicated custody situation. I scheduled my first consultation less than three weeks before my upcoming custody court date. This custody battle had been particularly challenging, especially after years of fighting alone while my children's father hired an attorney, which made me feel as though my voice was being overlooked. When I presented my case to Mr. Hogan, he quickly identified the necessary steps to address my concerns, as if he had been present at every court hearing.  He promptly took action and prepared the essential documents for court. Mr. Hogan exhibits no fear of opposing counsel; he stands confidently before a judge. His extensive knowledge, direct approach, and passion for his work were truly impressive. Mr. Hogan recognized the false accusations from the opposing party, but more importantly, he understood and seen the unconditional love I have for my children and my determination to ensure their safety. In a remarkably short time, he articulated everything I had struggled to convey to the court over the years. He not only offered guidance but also maintained communication throughout the process. He responds promptly to emails and phone calls and is a formidable presence in court. Having Mr. Hogan and his team by my side has been a tremendous blessing, and I have complete confidence in his abilities. I find solace in knowing that he will support my boys and I at every stage of this process. If you are facing a custody battle, Mr. Hogan is with no doubt, the attorney you want representing you. I sincerely appreciate all that you have done and continue to do for my sons and I. Lastly, I would like to express my appreciation for the entire team. His paralegal, Natalia, demonstrates exceptional knowledge and education, and her ability to empathize as a mother is particularly noteworthy. Front desk assistant, Cassandra, communicates effectively and maintains excellent organization. Furthermore, accountant Lexi produces detailed invoices with remarkable precision. My gratitude extends to the entire office of Thomas P. Hogan.

I cannot thank Mr. Hogan and his team enough for the incredible support and guidance I received throughout my long and challenging divorce process. From the very first consultation, it was clear that Mr. Hogan wasn’t just focused on the legal aspects, but truly cared about my well-being and emotional state. During what was an incredibly stressful and emotional time, he made me feel heard, understood, and supported every step of the way.

What stood out the most was Mr. Hogan's genuine compassion. The divorce was drawn out, and there were many difficult moments, but he was always there with calm, clarity, and encouragement. Mr. Hogan was patient with my concerns, answered all my questions thoroughly, and helped me navigate not just the legal complexities but the emotional challenges as well. I never felt alone in this process, and I truly appreciate how much he went above and beyond to make sure I understood my options and felt confident in the decisions I was making.

The legal expertise was also top-notch. Mr. Hogan was always well-prepared, strategic, and insightful. He ensured I knew exactly what to expect and helped me stay focused on the bigger picture, despite the ups and downs. I’m so grateful for his professionalism and dedication to my case.

If you're looking for a divorce lawyer who is not only knowledgeable and skilled but also compassionate and understanding, I cannot recommend Mr. Hogan enough. He helped me through one of the most difficult times in my life, and I’m forever grateful for his guidance and support.
Awesome team. They made it easy to understand, set me up properly and I didn't have to worry about a thing. Thank you team.

I switched to Thomas P. Hogan’s office after my previous lawyer kept missing meetings and was nearly impossible to reach. During my consultation, his team was on it, with a paralegal assisting and relaying information efficiently. What stood out was the immediate action—I wasn’t just given promises; I saw real movement on my case that same day. A refreshing experience compared to what I dealt with before. Highly recommend.

reviews.text: Now that I had the chance to see how he really works, I feel like all the other reviews are fake! An attorney who doesn’t check the court schedule and allows court dates to pass, who doesn’t go to the court to pick up new paperwork or check for new filings, ignores the clients wishes and completely bullies and fear-mongers their client into custody  agreements that are emotionally and psychologically negative for the child involved? That’s a lawyer that you don’t want. You also don’t want a lawyer who cozies up to the opposing lawyer for who-knows-what underhanded reason, and that even the judge clearly thinks is not representing the best interests of their client. Oh did I forget? A retainer that will be used with no receipt of where it went and no billing records. Phone caalls and requests for information ignored, evidence ignored and passed over… I just don’t see how these other reviews could possibly be real. Terrible experience that had to actually be terminated in the middle of a hearing for such bad representation. - addition - there’s no other reply you could have made other than to defend. But the truth stands for itself.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Dear Ms. Cotrone,

Thank you for sharing your experience. While I strongly disagree with your characterization of the representation provided, I respect your right to express your perspective.

As your attorney, my role was to guide you through the legal process with transparency, strategy, and advocacy grounded in law, not emotion. At every stage of your case, I provided recommendations based on the facts, your child’s best interests, and what would stand in a courtroom, even when those recommendations were difficult to hear.

Court deadlines were met, filings were tracked, and a trial was scheduled, and contrary to your claims, every key step was documented in written communication. The choices around custody and settlement were ultimately yours to make, and at no point were you forced or misled. I also want to clarify that professional conduct with opposing counsel is not “cozying up,” but a reflection of ethical practice and respect for courtroom procedure, essential to effective legal advocacy.

We are proud of the high standards we maintain in client communication, billing transparency, and courtroom readiness. While it’s unfortunate that this matter did not conclude under our continued representation, I sincerely hope you find legal support that aligns with your expectations moving forward.

To those reading: My commitment remains unchanged: to offer grounded legal counsel, fearless advocacy, and honest communication. Every client, every case, deserves nothing less.

reviews.text: I would highly recommend Michael Weimer as your attorney.  My 22 year old daughter and I live in Louisiana but we are from California.  My daughter was visiting family in the Oakdale area in October.  Out of youthful indiscretion, she did donuts in an empty parking lot.  A police officer saw her and ticketed her.  This is a misdemeanor offense and considered reckless driving.  She appeared in front of a judge in January via a Zoom meeting.  The judge referred her case to the public defender's office and set another court date for days later.  The public defender's office told us that we did not need to appear in court and someone would contact her.  No one contacted us before her scheduled court date and time.  This resulted in a bench warrant being issued for her arrest.  I contacted Michael Weimer's office.  They immediately contacted me back.  Michael assured me that under a certain penal code, that he could represent my daughter.  At the next court date, he got the bench warrant rescinded and filed for discovery (information that the DA's office had).  Another court date was set and when it came, the case was deferred and another court date set.  Michael suggested that my daughter take a California Safe Driving Course and a Life Skills Course before her next court date.  She did and Michael brought her completed course certificates with him to the next court date.  The DA dismissed her case to our utter relief and delight.  This was a hard lesson for my young adult daughter but Michael walked us through it.  He was incredibly communicative in a very timely manner.   He was kind and compassionate.  He was knowledgeable and had a good legal strategy that worked in our case.  I highly recommend Michael Weimer as an attorney.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you Teri for the kind words. I’m thrilled we were able to sort everything out for your daughter. All the best to you.

reviews.text:
I am writing to express my concerns regarding the legal services provided by Roth  Legal Services. Over a month ago, I hired Roth Legal services and made a payment, but unfortunately, communication has been severely lacking. My son has yet to meet with his attorney to discuss his case, and despite one brief conversation, there has been no follow-up or support.

To make matters worse, the lawyer did not appear in court, resulting in my son's court date being postponed. The only explanation I received was that he was waiting for additional evidence, yet it seems there has been no review of the current discovery packet.

With an upcoming court case this week, I am increasingly anxious about the lack of preparation and communication. It feels as though my son's future is not being prioritized, and I can't help but feel disappointed by the level of care provided. Given the circumstances, I believe a public defender may have offered a more attentive response.

I understand that law firms can become overwhelmed, but taking on more cases than one can handle is a disservice to clients. I hope this feedback serves as a constructive critique, and I plan to provide an update following the upcoming court date, should the attorney fulfill his obligations.

* Mr. Roth has helped my friends through countless endeavors and have nothing but high praise of him
* If you ever need competent, friendly and helpful, legal fortitude, look no further

As a beneficiary of a Special Needs Trust managed by Strategic Legal Group, my experience has been deeply disappointing. A trustee’s primary responsibility is to act in the best interest of the beneficiary, but I’ve consistently felt unsupported, ignored, and left in the dark.

Communication has been a significant issue. Emails and inquiries often go unanswered or are addressed only after long delays, making it incredibly difficult to move forward with important decisions. When responses do come, they are often incomplete or fail to acknowledge the concerns I’ve raised, leaving me frustrated and stuck.

Beyond the lack of responsiveness, the overall management of the trust has felt resistant rather than supportive. Instead of collaborating to meet my needs, I’ve experienced deflections, delays, and a lack of urgency for issues that are critical to my independence and well-being.

I’m sharing this review to highlight my experience for others who may be considering this firm for trust management. I would not recommend their services to anyone looking for a trustee who prioritizes the beneficiary and communicates effectively.

I've decided to close the book on this experience. I was referred to Gary by a friend. My accident was 3/8/23, I hired Gary 8/9/23 &  signed settlement paperwork 7/24/25.

A con for me is that I never met face to face with an attorney during that time except when I signed the settlement paperwork. Everything is done through letter, email or in the case of deposition prep a call. I believe these practices may have come from the 2020 COVID shutdown.

I never understood that this would be the path. When  I had questions I would first try to ride things out until the next communication as l am aware this process is a slow one but would eventually email in which I received a short, direct but prompt response from Gary.

I requested to give my deposition at the law offices instead of at home. I did so in a room all by myself with the depo attorney on zoom. It's an enormously overwhelming experience to go through alone. Again, I didn't expect that.

As far as Gary goes- I found him to be quite professional but only spoke to him via phone the day I hired him. I never met him. He was timely in responding to my emails. The deposition attorney was professional and thorough.

I think the streamline no fuss no muss practice of not going in to the office to speak live might work for some. But I also feel that while I understood a bit of how this works (I had a previous case I very stressfully yet successfully handled myself) I still felt out of the loop at times & there isn't anything quite like looking someone in the eye in regard to the life of your case, what to expect, strategy, etc. It's an extremely grueling process.

Finally, I need to mention how my case ended which is really why I am taking the time to write this review. I was contacted by Romina an attorney from Gary's office very late in the day to let me know there was a settlement offer. She very quickly went
over my options while I tried to scribble down everything she was saying. I was overwhelmed and eventually cried. She then offered that I could come to the office & meet with her. I did my research & prepared for that meeting with questions & concerns.

I was interested in Compromise and Release which was explained by Romina on the phone but not in paperwork to be signed. I recited back my understanding of my options. It appeared I wasn't offered the C&R because I had worked a few days very recently per diem & the insurance considered me
"employed" by them. I explained that I was simply helping them out and could easily stop doing so. Romina advised & recommended as it would get the ball rolling that I signed Stip with Request for Award & that she would speak with the other side in regard to my desire.

The other side agreed & paperwork was sent to me for C& R. However, after reading over and researching I had many concerns & questions. I sent Romina an email outlining all my concerns, etc. She responded back 11/3/25 stating she would have her office set an appointment .

Well, here we are this day and I never heard back from Romina's office. My concerns &  questions were extensive. I've been paid & am being paid, the attorney has been paid, case settled but not the way I requested. That matters.

My recommendation would be to anyone is to make sure you are seen. Face to face...so you are a person, not just a case. This stuff is important and complicated. You are entitled to your attorney's attention. You are entitled to feel seen. Do not sign anything leaving anything hanging. That was totally my bad. I trusted instead of going with my gut.

I'm good at standing up for myself and encouraging others to do so but from accident until signing this took 21/2 years of my life. I feel like I've quit because I didn't get what I requested which is TOTALLY against my personal practice but | just don't feel like I would get the outcome I originally requested without added stress. Who needs that in retirement?!

Laying this to rest.

They scheduled a QME appt and didn’t provide all the evidence (like the video showing what happened to me).  I was told by the QME on the day of my visit that he did not view the video because it wasn’t provided to him.  When I called the attorneys office Mr. Nelson was not available so I spoke to rep she didn’t have any idea what was happening with my case and when I asked why the videos was not sent to QME with supporting evidence she said she thought my employers attorney would be providing it.  It didn’t make sense as they are not working towards helping me.  When I finally did talk to Mr Nelson he made it sound like the videos is no big deal but did schedule another QME at which time he eventually sent the video for that appt. However when I tried to tell the QME about my pain he did not want to hear it.  Months pasted by  with no updates no communication after getting a low settlement offer in the mail I didn’t respond to that offer and I made the decision to tell Mr Nelson I didn’t want him to represent me and asked if I needed to fill out a form to remove him as my attorney he said no. A week later my employers attorney reached out to discuss closing the W/C claim with the same low offer but they called him since he was listed as my attorney on record it appears I did have to sign a form to have him removed.  Mr Nelson called me to discuss the offer I was surprised since the week prior I verbally told him I didn’t want him to represent me and he told me that I didn’t need to sign anything. Mr Nelson told me that the offer they talked to him about “ was a good offer” I told him I was not going to take the offer and reminded him I didn’t want him to represent me on this case.  At that time he sent me a form to release him as my attorney (which I don’t understand why he didn’t do that when I first told him that I did not want him to represent me). I signed  the release of representative but Mr Nelson now has a lien against me for $2000 +.for doing absolutely nothing on my W/C case.  I would NOT recommend this attorneys office to anyone.  I’ve seen his reviews and I question how legit they are I know for me I got stuck paying someone that doesn’t fight hard for me and then puts a lien on someone who is already struggling due to an injury cause at work.

reviews.text:
I hired Gary Nelson to represent me in a workplace injury lawsuit, and while there were some positives in my experience, there were also significant disappointments. Initially, Gary assured me of a successful outcome from our very first communication, and in all honesty, he was quite personable over the phone. He was usually prompt in responding to my emails, which I appreciated.

However, I was disappointed that he never met with me face-to-face to assess the severity of my injury and better understand how to push the case forward. The case took more than three years to reach a settlement, which felt excessively long. During this time, Gary was not always clear in communicating the stages of the case to me.

I was particularly unhappy when Gary sent an associate to represent him during my deposition. I had expected Gary to be there to prepare and support me through this crucial part of the process. When the case seemed to be dragging on, Gary suggested that I accept the settlement offered, which was far less than what I believed I deserved. Feeling disheartened by how the case was handled, I ultimately accepted the settlement.

Although the case is now closed, I still experience pain and minor issues related to my injury. Based on my experience, I would hesitate to recommend Gary Nelson for workplace injury cases. While he has some strengths, there were too many areas where his service fell short.
reviews.ownerResponse: Not sure if I represented this person or if they were paid by a competitor to attack me.

reviews.ownerResponse: Advice - when you seek legal advice let a law firm know what legal issue you might be having. reviews.text: Came here for a family law matter for court. The 3 lady’s that work in the office were great very sweet and attentive. Waisted almost a month on him and he decided to drop out of my case last minute with a upcoming hearing soon because I wouldn’t sign “limited scope of practice” when that was never what I hired him for. When I told him that’s not what was agreed on he replied “you are right you did say you didn’t want limited scope of practice”. When trying to explain what we had talked about he replied “You know what we are dropping out of your case right now”. Awful demeanor and yells for no reason.. To think this man was referred by another attorney for abuse cases. Cowardly way out but also a huge blessing. I was lucky to get the money back that I had given him to give another attorney. Please take your money and time elsewhere especially if you have a very sensitive child custody matter. Biggest advice ever listen to your gut..

reviews.text: Did some business with this law firm. Had a phone consult and spelled out exactly what I would like and was submitting an offer on a plot of land. No prices were discussed or contract agreed on with this law firm and I was not expecting a bill until we had done this. The firm passed me off the the paralegal who was not part of my consultation so did not have an understanding of what I was wanting on my offer. I received a document that seemed as though Triebsch took no notes or even paid attention. I had to email the paralegal and call multiple times because every letter I received was wrong and was not I had asked for. I would have been better off having chat GPT draft my offer as it was an informal letter and having the attorney review the final deal. I received a bill not only for my conversation but I was billed for every single email I sent stating that what I had asked for was incorrect. I called to speak with the attorney to review the bill and he was supposed to call me back. He ended up having his book keeper call me back and state “pay whatever and take your business elsewhere”. I was polite on my phone call and was treated poorly. I do have much more business and another property I am in the middle of purchasing and am glad I did not continue with this attorney. I would have not have had a problem paying for the consult and the drafted document. All I needed was a call back to go over it and also a way to pay the bill as there was no way listed for how they accept payments. “This is how we have always done business” is a poor thing to say.

reviews.text:
Do not hire this attorney.  Impossible to get ahold of.  Will not return your call even after a message is left. Sat in court and heard another of her clients have the same issue.  The judge will have to motivate her to get her job done. Have been waiting on a probate matter to be resolved and is still more than a year.  The property has yet to be placed on the trust as per county records.  She will not answer your questions unless you are her client when it comes to "trust" matters.  Her words to me were " I am (jane does) attorney and not yours,  I am here to protect my client"  instead of the trust which her client represents.  Yet the money to pay for her services comes from the trust essentially my pocket.  DO NOT HIRE UNLESS YOU WANT YOUR CASE TO TAKE YEARS.  Seasoned attorneys will get the same job done in 3 to 4 months total time.   Hope this helps.

Ps. I'm still waiting for the trust to show it's expense report.  By the way she has been on the case for 1 yr.

Update.  Just got the itemized expense report.  With no documents to support ( no bank statement).  I guess it's expected to pay for services rendered without any supporting documents.

The Law Office of Kaycee Matulac provided exceptional service throughout the entire probate process for my father’s estate. From the initial consultation to the final discharge, their expertise and professionalism were evident at every step. Tina Glow, their legal assistant, was particularly attentive and responsive, keeping me informed and making a complex legal process feel manageable.

What impressed me most was their thoroughness and attention to detail—they ensured all documentation was properly filed, deadlines were met, and the distribution of assets was handled exactly according to legal requirements. The court approved everything without issues, which speaks to their competence.

Their fees were reasonable and transparent, with no surprises. They expertly guided me through my responsibilities as administrator and made sure I understood what was happening at each stage.

If you’re facing the daunting task of probate administration, I couldn’t recommend the Law Office of Kaycee Matulac more highly. They turned what could have been an overwhelming experience into a straightforward process with a successful conclusion.

reviews.text:
My experience with Vivian Ann Mejia was not good. I was told that she was the best in Stanislaus County when it comes to mitigating DUIs. And yes, I truly believe that she may be an intelligent woman and an expert at what she does. However, there were a few things that made uncomfortable:

1) She seems to be very inundated with her caseloads, which is completely understandable. However, if this is the case, then she should have been honest and simply stated that she is unable to take on my DUI case. And I could have taken my business elsewhere to an attorney who is more attentive. I say that because there were several times where she promised to return my phone calls, text-messages, or emails, and did not follow through on her word to do so. In turn, I felt the need to stay on top of her (more than probably should be) in order to make sure that she was on point in handling my case. Poor Communication style.

2) When I finally had an opportunity to meet with Vivian at her office, I wanted to converse with her on some things regarding my DUI case. In addition, I wanted to actually witness her style in person to fully gage her suitability for me. However, she seemed to be in a rush to leave the office. Yes, she set me up at one of her computers so I can review my traffic stop footage on my own, but again, she was not attentive. At this point I determined that this client-attorney relationship may not be good for me to continue. Again, I understand full schedules and been busy, but it was not a good way to start off a client-attorney relationship, especially when meeting in-person for the first time. First impression was not the best.

3) There were no written 'Terms & Conditions' for me to review in regard to how/ if I would be charged continuously for frequent communication. Unfortunately, I did not find this out until after I terminated Vivian's services. At this point, I was informed that she would initiate an itemization process toward an issued refund. Long story short, out of the $3,000 I paid out to her, only $159.00 was refunded. Considering that she did not step foot into any court setting on my behalf, this refund was beyond unreasonable. Aside from a 10 to 15 minute DMV hearing (via phone call), she did not perform any other significant work on my behalf that would be deserving of $3,000. No clarity.

Overall, since I just so happen to be an understanding and empathetic individual, I will exercise basic grace for what she lacked with me. As far the money that I lost, I am trusting that she will correct this issue, hopefully sooner than later. Other than that, I feel that "2 stars" should be fair at this time.
reviews.ownerResponse:
We take all reviews seriously and appreciate the opportunity to address the concerns raised.

Our firm is built on a strong foundation of professionalism, transparency, and clear communication. To that end, we maintain strict policies and procedures to ensure that each client’s case is handled efficiently, with all necessary documentation and communication being fully documented.

Regarding the concerns about communication, our firm adheres to a well-structured process for managing client interactions. This matter included over 70 text messages, 20 emails, numerous phone calls, and a Zoom meeting to ensure clients were kept informed. While we recognize that different clients have different communication preferences, it is important to note that all communication was handled within the framework of our standard procedures.

We want to clarify that with regards to fees there are many factors and actions taken for every case. The total fee was $4500, with a deposit of $2500 paid in July 2024. Our agreement included structured payments with a clear understanding of when services would be billed. The client failed to adhere to the agreed-upon payment schedule, which was due starting in August. Despite this, we did not charge any late fees and worked to ensure services continued based on the initial agreement.



The client’s dissatisfaction with the refund is noted, but it is important to recognize that the amount of $2840 was billed for services already rendered. This includes substantial work such as reviewing all reports and electronic evidence, subpoenaing essential evidence, conducting the DMV hearing, numerous communications with the client, and preparing for negotiations with the DA.

Given the work completed before court proceedings, and considering industry standards, the charge of $2840 reflects the value of services provided.

The refund issued was in line with our policy, which accounts for the work performed and the out-of-pocket expenses incurred, such as MVARS disc and mailing fees. It’s standard practice in the legal industry to withhold charges for work completed in good faith before refunding any remaining balance. The client was refunded $159 after all services and fees were accounted for.

It’s important for clients to understand that legal services involve complex, time-consuming work, and expectations regarding refunds should be aligned with the work done and the agreements in place. We stand by the professionalism and thoroughness of the work performed and are committed to operating with integrity.

Thank you for your feedback, and we remain dedicated to upholding the highest standards possible.

If you are considering legal services, please reach out today to schedule an initial consultation.

Thank You,
Vivian A. Mejia | Law Office of Vivian A. Mejia

reviews.text:
I will acknowledge that Mr. Aguiano and his staff are tough.
But if we are all sincere, this is what we want . I had a very difficult case going on for three years, and Mr. Aguiano successfully helped me throughout my case. I was told by many others that my case was beyond belief, I lived through it and managed to come out of my case with success.
Several lawyers were hired in my case from the opposite party yet I stuck to one that stood by me and guided me the whole way through.
Admiring someone’s personality is big. I agree I am one to go with compassion and kindness with everything I do.
But when you want things done, and you recognize that it’s a dog eat dog world , you want someone that’s going to represent you to the fullest .
I agree some things are not the way that we want, but this is the way the world works . But as far as my case and how difficult it was, Mr. Angiano and his staff jumped all the loops and filled all the cracks to help me. My case was prolonged because it was one thing after another all my battles were hard , difficult and tired some. I believe we hire a lawyer not to like him or to be friends, but to get the job done.
This is what they did for me.!
I am highly and most grateful for their work. My case was long , difficult and unbelievable. Yet after years, I walked out with success. I’m still battling some situations that I will without a doubt contact him if I need so in the future.
Thank you, Mr. Angiano and staff !
My best wishes for you and your families.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you so much for your thoughtful and heartfelt review. We truly appreciate your trust and kind words. It was an honor to stand by you through such a challenging case, and we’re grateful for the opportunity to help you achieve a successful outcome. Wishing you all the best moving forward — we’re always here if you ever need us again.

reviews.text: I would not recommend this attorney to anyone. When I first needed representation I looked around and saw Richard on google with a good 4/5 star review. I read his 5/5 and his 1/5 and thought that the 1/5 stars reviews were just upset at him because the case was lost, but in fact that’s not the reason why. He only seemed to care in the beginning when I first called about my case, showing interest and telling me that he can be the one to help me out. So I decided to give the benefit of the doubt, and hired him. As soon as I sent the money over, everything immediately changed. When I had questions about the case and texted him, he would leave me on delivered for days often leading me to have to double text him for a response. In those responses he did not seem to care and would make it seem like a “search it up/ask someone else” response. He never truly cared about representation which is very disappointing to me, he only cares about the money he can earn. Also he would show up late to my hearings often 10-30 minutes late, showing me that he really doesn’t care about what he’s doing in general. Maybe the 5/5 stars reviews are when he still cared about representation and had that hunger in him to be the best attorney, but in my opinion if you are reading this. Do not hire him, it is much better to go to someone else. I gave him the benefit of the doubt and he still failed. While in court when he was negotiating with the DA it seemed like he didn’t care to really push for a better plea deal. He just told me straight forward “thats the best I can do” and he may not be lying but it would be nice to see you try a little harder through that door when negotiating. You failed at representing me Richard especially when I had hope in you.
reviews.ownerResponse: It's difficult to respond to your complaints when you do not use your real name or provide specifics about your case.  You are entitled to your opinion and I accept your criticism even when I disagree with it.  You clearly are unhappy with a plea arrangement.  As I explained to you, as I do with all of my clients, plea bargaining is only one option.  Jury trial is another.  It was your choice. It is my practice to go into great detail with every client on the benefits and risks of going to trial.   If I advised you to settle with a plea bargain rather than go to trial, it's because trial would have resulted in a more severe outcome.    I stand by my standards of practice and regret that you are dissatisfied.

reviews.text:
When we first enlisted Rick on my son's behalf, we paid a flat fee for achieving resolution.  Even though the case dragged on for over three years, there was never a request for additional funds.  Every step where legal advice or representation was required, it was handled with professionalism and courtesy.  No promises of an easy result were made, only that he would stick with us until everything was resolved.

I am saddened to see the negative reviews on here.  They do not represent the person, and lawyer, that we came to know over that three-year period.  People who are bitter about their circumstance appear to be lashing out at the easiest target, because they might not have received the "get out of jail free" card that they expected.

I have nothing but good to say about how Rick handled a tough and complex situation, and my son was eventually absolved of all responsibility after several changes of prosecutor and attempts by the DA's office to bully him into accepting unwarranted felony charges.  Today, he is free and able to return to his normal life with no criminal record or stigma, thanks to Richard Meyer.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thanks Gregg.  I really your comments.  That was a long road but I'm glad we finally got the dismissal.

reviews.text: Hi, if you are looking for an attorney who will speak, and fight  on your behalf or your loved ones behalf. Don’t I repeat Don’t choose Richard A. Meyer. Please  listen to me when I say he won’t speak up he won’t argue your cause. He will do nothing to assist. Especially if you’re African- American He will tell you that he will communicate with your family, and he won’t. Even if you have paperwork or letters of support for your loved one  he will not accept them. He will lie and say he didn’t receive the email all the while you have proof that he did. If you ask him to ask The district attorney or the judge for specific things on your love ones behalf he will lie and say he did it while you you’re  sitting in the court room seeing, and hearing that he did not ask for it. His lack of interest in his profession is so bad that the judge had to ask him was he going to speak up on my son’s behalf. No judge ever has to / should ever have to ask an attorney if they’re going to speak up on their clients behalf.An attorney already knows what they are there to do they choose  this profession. So if you choose RICHARD A. MEYER who chose this profession but refuses to do the job. Or picks, and chooses who he wants  to handle/argue their case properly according to their skin color. You’d be choosing someone who shouldn’t be able to practice law because he doesn’t work by the oath that he took.
reviews.ownerResponse:
I am familiar with your son's case which is the basis of your review. The accusations you make in your post are absolutely shameful and 100% false. You claim that I am prejudice against African-Americans. This could not be further from the truth as anyone who knows me will tell you. Your son was charged with very serious crimes that could have resulted in his incarceration for decades. I represented your son to the best of my ability and negotiated a resolution that was approved by your son and it was one that he was completely satisfied with. It was his choice to take his case to jury trial or settle it through a negotiated resolution. I think he made the right decision. You also claimed that I failed to make the appropriate argument at your son's bail review hearing. You and your other son stood in the hallway telling me that I failed to ask the court to consider a less restrictive means of detention like an ankle bracelet. You both were incorrect. I ordered the transcript of that hearing and emailed it to you and even highlighted the passage where I did in fact request the ankle bracelet (electronic monitoring program). You clearly misunderstood the proceedings.
In addition, I spoke to you after nearly every court hearing, and there were many, and we spoke on the phone countless times. It was only after your relentless insistence on arguing about every last word I said to you, that I discontinued briefing you in the hall after court. However, even after your constant barrage of rude comments, I emailed you updates after each court hearing although I had no obligation to do so because I was and am sympathetic to any parent stressing over their incarcerated child. The allegations you have made in your review are untrue and it appears that you are simply bent on hurting my law practice. We spoke several times after you son's case resolved about other difficult circumstances you were dealing with. You are being disingenuous in your review.
As a parent, I understand the stress you were going through at the time and I regret that my legal representation of your son was not to your satisfaction. That being said, it is unfair of you to disparage my character with false allegations of racism in the way that you have here.

After having struggled with two public defenders in a serious case with multiple felony charges, I was blessed to have the services of Attorney Rebecka A. Monez. She was on top of the case from the very beginning and was so professional throughout the whole process. Her dedication to my case showed when even in the midst of dealing with personal grief, due to the passing of her mother, she never lost her ground and in a matter of six months got every single charge dismissed where the two previous attorneys only attempted to have me take a plea deal. I highly recommend Attorney Monez because her fine work allowed me to keep my clean record and avoid deportation. Really, no words can express how grateful I am for having an excellent attorney go above and beyond for my cause. With her you’re not just a dollar sign, she sees the person, the human being and our vulnerability when we face the system. I would trust no other attorney in the Modesto area for complicated cases that could put a life at risk.

Once again Attorney Rebecka A. MONEZ thank you for your help.

Sincerely:
Cosme Eduardo Gil 🙏🏻

For everyone who is facing the difficult decision of filing for Bankruptcy, my husband and I strongly, and highly recommend Flor Tataje and her amazing team at "Tataje Law Office in Modesto, Ca.  We were beyond stressed with overwhelming debt that we hardly slept, we had no way of life, just work and pay bills, until we decided to put an end to all the financial stress and agreed to file for BK, after reading so many positive reviews and so many satisfied clients, we decided without hesitation to be represented by Flor, and we are so glad we did. They will guide you every step of the way from start to finish, you'll never feel alone, and most importantly you'll be able to breathe again.
We can't express it enough how happy we are to have had Flor, and her team represent us, they have been truly a blessing.
Again, thank you so much for everything.
Blanca Z.
